meaning: vt.使屈服,征服,使服从,克制,抑制
examples: As one ceases to subjugate themselves, they will cease to be subordinate.
They are willing to subjugate their own feelings for the good of the group.
Earth is no longer willing to subjugate herself to the human dance beloved!
If the Serbian factions undertake to subjugate Croatia militarily, victory -- if only a pyrrhic one -- will likely go to Belgrade.
"A lot of it is women who, because it is difficult to get on, subjugate their own ambition into their kids, " she said.
